narrative: Jindal again led on absolute waste generation at 1.58 million MT, while Tata had the highest absolute waste at 16.83 million MT. Tata led total waste recovered at 18.88 million MT, followed by JSW at 15.08 million MT and SAIL at 14.37 million MT, indicating that Tata’s circularity engine remained strong even though absolute waste was high. The clearest laggard was Tata on disposal, with 307,577 MT disposed, mainly due to 303,496 MT landfilled, whereas SAIL reported the lowest disposal at 2,358 MT. Jindal was strongest on turnover-based waste intensity, while JSW led physical-output intensity at 0.7 MT/tcs. Tata’s FY2024 profile was therefore mixed: strong recovery leadership, but weak landfill/disposal performance. Tata’s disclosed practice of recycling/reusing waste or selling co-products to industries such as cement is strategically important, but the FY2024 landfill number shows execution or timing issues.
